what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600×900 HTML slide comparing investment terms and a capped cash-out window in two side-by-side cards, with blueprint corner texture, a thin hatched footer, and a small V mark.
observations: Static single-file HTML document with inline CSS and no JavaScript.
Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Main canvas is a centered 16:9 .view with absolute-positioned title, card grid, footer band, and mark.
Left card presents instrument and round terms: $500k goal, $5.5M pre-money valuation, capped convertible note, cheque size, and partner room.
Right card presents capped cash-out terms: 91× multiple, 5-year horizon, $6.5M buy-back valuation, optional return near 18%, and a twelve-month window.
CSS comments encode design constraints such as blueprint underlay, title treatment, plate heading style, and footer behavior.
A later override block shrinks the footer band to a quiet 28px hatch, hides its clock/copy content, and lifts the V mark.
standouts: Two-card comparison structure with warm and cool card backgrounds.
Masked blueprint grids appear only in the top corners as low-opacity underlay.
Footer content exists in markup but is visually hidden by v2.1 correction CSS.
conventions: Uses semantic-lite class names for visual roles such as .view, .cards, .card, .statrow, .box, .frag, and .gband.
Relies heavily on design tokens via CSS custom properties.
Uses absolute positioning and fixed pixel canvas dimensions rather than fluid layout.
Uses inline CSS comments as local design-spec annotations.
Breaks the earlier footer styling through later !important overrides instead of replacing the original rule.
concerns: The comment says the surface values are self-contained, but the file depends on /surface/styles/vars.css for CSS variables.
The footer clock and label remain in the DOM while being hidden by CSS overrides.
Several !important overrides make the final footer behavior dependent on cascade order.
Fixed 1600×900 dimensions do not adapt to smaller viewports within this file.
notes: A static presentation slide for comparing funding terms, with visible design-rule comments and a corrective footer override.
questions: Are the CSS variables in /surface/styles/vars.css guaranteed to be available wherever this file is rendered?
Should the hidden footer copy remain in the markup as retained source context or be removed from this variant?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and content indicate a side-by-side terms comparison, and the rendered structure matches that stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Quiet correction of decorative footer treatment
intention_signals: It seems intended to present investment terms as a polished two-card slide while preserving explicit design constraints and correcting an earlier footer treatment in place.
novelty: The file embeds design-governance comments directly beside the CSS rules and keeps a visible correction layer through cascade overrides.
contribution: Contributes a token-driven 16:9 terms-comparison slide pattern with paired cards, restrained blueprint corner texture, compact financial stat boxes, triangular bullet fragments, and a muted hatched footer treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS 16:9 slide showing two side-by-side cards for investment terms and capped cash-out details with blueprint corner accents, tokenized styling, and a corrected thin footer hatch.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting funding terms in a controlled visual system while documenting and enforcing local design treatment decisions.
